This survey should take you no longer than 10 minutes to complete and will ask you about the following:
 
  • Your reasons for leaving;
  • Your role;
  • Your supervisor;
  • Your team;
  • Your compensation; and
  • The district as a whole
 
We want to get your feedback while it’s fresh, so please take the survey as soon as possible, ideally in one sitting. Simply answer as honestly as you can, and if you don't know the answer to any of the questions, you can leave them blank.

Factors involved in your decision to leave the district.  

We know people decide to leave jobs based on lots of different factors.  We'd like to understand what extent each of the factors below had a bearing on your leaving.
We know people decide to leave jobs based on lots of different factors. To what extent were each of the below a factor in your decision to leave?

column10: ["English I, locally-developed exam, 90% ","English II, EOC, level V Scale Score ≥ 565","NC Math 1, EOC,  level V Scale Score ≥ 563","NC Math 2, locally-developed exam, 90%","NC Math 3, EOC, level V Scale Score ≥ 563","Biology, EOC, level V Scale Score ≥ 361","Spanish, French, German, Proficiency Exam, Novice High","BB40 Business Management I","BF21 Financial Planning I","GS11 Project Management I","FI21 Interior Design Fundamentals","FN41 Food and Nutrition I","HU40 Health Science I"]

column6:
I understand this assessment will include a two-phase process.

Phase 1 will include an examination to establish my mastery of the foundational skills and content that this course requires.  
I must earn a minimum score on Phase 1 to move forward to Phase 2.  The minimum score for each type of assessment is as follows: 
90% for a locally-developed exam
90% for a CTE Post-Assessment
Novice High for World Language Assessments
Level V on an EOC
I have one attempt at the exam.
Prior to the exam, I may review the content standards for the course or subject. Standards can be found at this link: http://www.ncpublicschools.org/curriculum/
If I earn the appropriate score, I will move onto Phase 2.  If not, I will not have the opportunity to earn CDM for this course. Results of Phase 1 cannot be appealed. 


In Phase 2, I will participate in an assessment that reflects a deep understanding of the content standards, including the ability to apply the skills and knowledge expected at the end of the course.  Phase 2 may require a presentation, project, on-demand writing, performance assessment, or interview as evidence of my abilities.
The CDM Review Panel will determine if I have demonstrated mastery of the course content and skills.  If I am successful, I will get credit for the course which will appear on my transcript as “CDM.”  No grade or quality points will be granted; this process will have no impact on my GPA. Appeals for unsuccessful attempts on Phase 2 must be submitted to the school based CDM coordinator, in writing, within 10 school days of notification of results.
I understand that I will meet with my school’s CDM Coordinator to discuss the process and long-term implications.
Confirmation
column7: By clicking the box below I agree to the above statement.
